summary: Backend refactoring Phase 7 testing complete - All Doula and Midwife routes migrated from server.py to routes/doula.py and routes/midwife.py are working correctly. 36/36 tests passed.
critical_code_review_comments: Modular doula router migration successful - routes/doula.py properly integrated via dependencies.py pattern
Modular midwife router migration successful - routes/midwife.py properly integrated via dependencies.py pattern
All doula routes correctly prefixed with /api/doula through APIRouter(prefix='/doula') and api_router inclusion
All midwife routes correctly prefixed with /api/midwife through APIRouter(prefix='/midwife') and api_router inclusion
Role-based access control working - Doula endpoints require DOULA role, Midwife endpoints require MIDWIFE role (return 403 for MOM)
Auth routes regression verified - /api/auth/login and /api/auth/me still work correctly
Mom routes regression verified - /api/mom/profile and /api/mom/team still work correctly
Provider routes regression verified - /api/provider/clients and /api/provider/dashboard still work correctly
MOCKED subscription endpoints still functional from Phase 5 migration
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend refactoring Phase 7 complete. Doula routes at /api/doula/* now served from modular router (routes/doula.py). Midwife routes at /api/midwife/* now served from modular router (routes/midwife.py). All endpoints verified for: profile (GET/PUT), onboarding, dashboard, and contract-defaults (doula only). Auth routes (Phase 4), Subscription routes (Phase 5), Mom routes (Phase 6), and Provider routes continue to work correctly. Server.py reduced from 6,599 to 6,483 lines.

doula_profile.note: Returns user_id and profile data; requires DOULA role

doula_profile_update.note: Updates profile fields like bio, experience_years

doula_onboarding.note: Creates/updates doula profile; marks onboarding complete

doula_dashboard.note: Returns active_clients, total_clients, pending_contracts, unpaid_invoices, upcoming_appointments, unread_messages

midwife_profile.note: Returns user_id and profile data; requires MIDWIFE role

midwife_profile_update.note: Updates profile fields like bio, experience_years, credentials

midwife_onboarding.note: Creates/updates midwife profile; marks onboarding complete

midwife_dashboard.note: Returns prenatal_clients, active_clients, total_clients, pending_contracts, unpaid_invoices, upcoming_visits, upcoming_appointments, unread_messages

architecture_notes.modular_routes: Doula routes migrated to /app/backend/routes/doula.py with prefix='/doula'; Midwife routes migrated to /app/backend/routes/midwife.py with prefix='/midwife'
architecture_notes.dependencies: Shared dependencies via /app/backend/routes/dependencies.py - uses get_current_user(), check_role(), db, get_now()
architecture_notes.role_based_access: Doula endpoints require DOULA role via check_role(['DOULA']); Midwife endpoints require MIDWIFE role via check_role(['MIDWIFE']) - returns 403 for unauthorized roles
architecture_notes.server_reduction: Server.py reduced from 6,599 to 6,483 lines after doula/midwife routes migration

mocked_apis_list: Subscription endpoints remain MOCKED (start-trial, activate, validate-receipt)
mocked_apis.note: Doula and Midwife routes use real database queries; subscription routes remain mocked
